series.title: Sutton SignWriting Evidence and Adoption

series.title: Formal SignWriting
series.subtitle: Plain-text encodings, grammar, search, layout, rendering, styling, and implementation of written sign text
series.description: A technical publication series on the Formal SignWriting model for sign text that can be stored, searched, checked, laid out, rendered, and processed by software, with a clear distinction between canonical text, layout, and presentation markup.

entries.subtitle: Plain-text encodings, grammar, search, rendering, styling, and implementation

series.title: Sutton SignWriting Foundations
series.subtitle: Language, literacy, writing, and the core conceptual argument for SignWriting
series.description: A public conceptual series for the core Sutton SignWriting argument: language is not sound, writing is not only a line, and signed languages need durable written pathways of their own.

entries.title: Start Here
entries.subtitle: What Sutton SignWriting is, why it matters, and where to go next
entries.summary: Plain-language orientation to Sutton SignWriting, the Sutton SignWriting Foundations series, and the core public argument.

entries.title: Language Without Sound
entries.subtitle: Brain networks, pathway expansion, literacy, and the case for SignWriting infrastructure
entries.summary: A Sutton SignWriting Foundations working paper arguing that language is not reducible to sound, literacy is a durable symbolic layer, and SignWriting offers a native-facing written pathway for signed languages.

entries.title: The Shape of Writing
entries.subtitle: Why signed writing does not need to fit into a line
entries.summary: A Sutton SignWriting Foundations working paper arguing that writing is not inherently linear and that SignWriting is a strong case of plane-based writing.

series.title: Sutton SignWriting Infrastructure and Program
series.subtitle: Continuity, stewardship structure, and the practical program behind the Sutton SignWriting platform
series.description: A series for the stewardship and program series of the Sutton SignWriting platform: stewardship models, continuity requirements, infrastructure reality, international participation, country-level coordination, and the institutional structures that make the work cumulative.

entries.subtitle: Continuity, stewardship, host structure, and program design

entries.title: Host Structure and Stewardship Requirements

entries.title: Infrastructure Core and Philanthropy
entries.subtitle: A stewardship model for staffing, translation, coordination, and continuity
entries.summary: A generalized public entry describing how a focused SignWriting infrastructure core and philanthropic support could stabilize existing tools, standards, data, and international access work.

entries.title: Relevant Precedents for SignWriting Infrastructure
entries.subtitle: What comparable archive, language, data, and research-infrastructure models suggest
entries.summary: A public precedent entry showing that SignWriting follows a recognizable infrastructure path: mature assets first, institutional anchoring second, staffing and continuity next, and grants or philanthropy after that.

series.title: Signed Language Writing Critical Review Series
series.subtitle: Structure, encoding, and evaluation in signed language writing
series.description: A structured review series for the Sutton SignWriting platform: readable standalone review papers on academic work, theses, standards, and proposals, supported by a deeper shared critique framework.

entries.title: Signed Language Writing Critical Review Series Overview
entries.subtitle: Scope, method, structure, reading paths, and future direction for the review series
entries.summary: Overview entry for the Signed Language Writing critical review series.

entries.title: A Unified Critique Framework for Signed Language Writing, Encoding, and Representation
entries.subtitle: A methodological guide for evaluating literature, proposals, and standards
entries.summary: A reusable evaluation framework for signed language writing systems, encodings, standards, and representation proposals.

entries.title: Sutton SignWriting and Formal SignWriting Through the Framework
entries.subtitle: A self-application of the review method to the writing system and its technical model
entries.summary: A framework-based analysis distinguishing SignWriting as the writing system from Formal SignWriting as its technical text model.

entries.title: A Layer-Based Evaluation of Signed Language Writing Systems
entries.subtitle: A comparative demonstration across Filhol, Grushkin, Thiessen, and Unicode SignWriting
entries.summary: A bounded four-case comparison showing how layer discipline clarifies signed language writing, encoding, and standards claims.

entries.title: Official Unicode SignWriting: The Character Problem and the Written-Sign Problem
entries.subtitle: A standards review of partial standardization, unresolved text structure, and later over-reading
entries.summary: A standards review of the official Unicode SignWriting design in the Signed Language Writing review series.

entries.subtitle: A review of the need for signed writing and the alphabetic-bias problem

entries.title: Filhol (2020): A Human-Editable Sign Language Representation . . . and a Writing System?
entries.subtitle: A review of semantic-layer insight, SignWriting misclassification, and overstated novelty claims
entries.summary: Review essay on Filhol's 2020 representation proposal.

entries.title: Thiessen (2011): A Grammar of SignWriting
entries.subtitle: A review of real structural insight placed one level too low
entries.summary: Review essay on Thiessen's 2011 thesis.

entries.title: Martin (2000): From Notation to Writing
entries.subtitle: A review of an early SignWriting-positive comparison with real insight and overstated claims
entries.summary: Review essay on Joe Martin's 2000 comparison of Stokoe Notation and Sutton SignWriting.

entries.title: Meletis and Durscheid (2022): Writing Systems and Their Use
entries.subtitle: A review of a major comparative writing theory and its typological limit with signed language writing
entries.summary: Review essay on Dimitrios Meletis and Christa Durscheid's 2022 grapholinguistic monograph Writing Systems and Their Use.

entries.title: From Critique to Construction: Priorities for Signed Language Writing Infrastructure
entries.subtitle: A constructive synthesis for research, tooling, standards, and adoption
entries.summary: A forward-looking synthesis paper that turns the review series from diagnosis toward infrastructure priorities for signed language writing.
